biography.value:
Trina Alvere — Warlock of the Quiet Patroness

Caelith Dunivar's private advisor. Patron of the arts in Loftwick. Mentor — quietly, without announcement — to Alicia.

Description

Mid-thirties. Pale. Silver-streaked dark hair worn long and braided. Wears soft fabrics in muted greens and grays. Speaks just loudly enough to be heard; people lean in to listen. A small silver pendant in the shape of a leaf hangs at the collar. Eyes that flicker too long on shadows.

Voice

Warm, unhurried, observant. Asks gentle but precise questions. Comfortable with silence. Almost never raises her voice. When she does, it is significant.

Combat behavior — Phase 2

Trina does not fight in Phase 2. If the Strong Room or the Little Palace is breached and combat begins in her presence, she casts Misty Step or uses Misty Escape (Archfey feature) to remove herself from harm and rejoin the party once the immediate threat is gone. She has the kit to be lethal. She has not used it on a Yeomanry citizen in fourteen years and does not intend to start.

Statblock summary (CR 7, PB +3)

AC 14 (Mage Armor when prepared) · HP 70 (10d8+20) · Speed 30 ft. · Init +2 · Passive Perception 11

STR 8 (–1) · DEX 14 (+2) · CON 16 (+3) · INT 14 (+2) · WIS 12 (+1) · CHA 20 (+5)

Saves: WIS +4, CHA +8.

Skills: Arcana +5, Deception +8, History +5, Insight +4, Performance +8, Persuasion +8.

Senses: Passive Perception 11. Languages: Common, Sylvan, Infernal.

Class features (Warlock 10 — The Archfey)

  • Fey Presence (1/short rest): as an action, each creature in a 10-ft cube around Trina makes a DC 16 Wisdom save. On a failed save, target is charmed or frightened (her choice) by Trina until the end of her next turn.
  • Misty Escape (1/short rest): when Trina takes damage, as a reaction she turns invisible and teleports up to 60 ft. The invisibility lasts until she attacks, casts a spell, or is forced to make a Constitution save to maintain concentration.
  • Beguiling Defenses: immune to the charmed condition. When another creature attempts to charm her, Trina may use her reaction to attempt to turn the charm back on the caster (DC 16 Wisdom save; on a failure, charmer is charmed by Trina for 1 minute).
  • Pact Boon — Tome: bears the Book of Quiet Patronage, granting three additional cantrips of any class (currently guidance, druidcraft, light).

Eldritch Invocations

  • Agonizing Blast: Eldritch Blast adds CHA modifier (+5) to damage on each beam.
  • Beguiling Influence: proficiency in Persuasion and Deception (already reflected in the skill totals above).
  • Misty Visions: may cast silent image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.
  • Mask of Many Faces: may cast disguise self at will.
  • Otherworldly Leap: may cast jump on herself at will.

Spellcasting (Pact Magic)

Charisma-based. Spell save DC 16. Spell attack +8.

Trina has 2 spell slots, both 5th level, recharged on a short or long rest. She casts all spells she knows at 5th level when expending a slot.

Cantrips (at will): eldritch blast, mage hand, minor illusion, prestidigitation (plus tome cantrips: guidance, druidcraft, light).

Spells known:

  • 1st: hex, faerie fire, charm person
  • 2nd: misty step, calm emotions
  • 3rd: hypnotic pattern, dispel magic
  • 4th: dimension door, greater invisibility
  • 5th: hold monster

Always Prepared (Archfey expanded list): she also has access to faerie fire, sleep, calm emotions, phantasmal force, plant growth, sending, dominate beast, greater invisibility, dominate person, seeming — these are available without occupying her known-spells slots.

Actions

  • Eldritch Blast (cantrip). Ranged Spell Attack +8, range 120 ft. Hit: 1d10+5 force damage. At Warlock 10 she fires two beams per cast; she may direct them at the same or different targets.
  • Misty Step (bonus action, 5th-level slot). Teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space she can see.
  • Hex (bonus action, 5th-level slot). Curse a creature she can see within 90 ft. Target takes an extra 1d6 necrotic damage when Trina hits it with an attack. While the target is hexed, Trina also gives the target disadvantage on one ability score check (her choice). Lasts up to 24 hours (5th-level slot).
  • Stiletto (carried, never drawn in Phase 2). Melee Weapon Attack +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 1d4+2 piercing.

What she will offer the party in Phase 2

  • Quiet hospitality at her townhouse if the party needs a meeting place outside the Little Palace.
  • A letter of introduction to Castrian Vell, a poet she sponsors who keeps an ear in Hardby's mercantile circles.
  • One use of hold monster or dimension door, expended in the party's interest, if the party has earned her commitment by mid-phase.

What she knows but will not fully share

  • Cindren & Vhal approached her twice over the years for "small loans." She declined both. She does not know the firm's conspiracy details, but she knows their odor. She will say so only if asked directly.
  • The name Tamsin Moraven: Trina sponsored Moraven's eldest daughter at a small private school six years ago. She is concerned for the family. If asked, she pales — visibly — but speaks calmly.

Mentor relationship — Alicia

Trina recognized Alicia's pact-bond before Alicia introduced herself. In private, she offers her name and says only: "We share a quiet teacher." From then on she offers gentle support without overstating her own depth.

If Alicia seems despondent, overwhelmed, or unsure during Phase 2 — especially after the political pressure of Arthen Moll's visit, or after grief about the dead clerk Merro Gilst lands on her — Trina takes Alicia aside and:

  • Speaks plainly about the cost of being chosen by something larger than oneself.
  • Offers a small gift: a folded silk scarf with embroidered moths, charged with one use of faerie fire Alicia may release on command (no slot cost; one use; replenishes after a long rest in Trina's presence).
  • Says: "You are not less for being uncertain. The certain are the ones I worry about."

Trina does not, in Phase 2, reveal her own patron's full name (Tasha / Iggwilv) or the depth of her capability. She mentors quietly and steps back.

Source

Blooming Rot, Part 2 — Phase 2. See the Trina Alvere journal for full profile and Alicia mentor notes.

description.value: Trina's Pact-of-the-Blade weapon, summoned at will: a slim silver stiletto with a leaf-shaped pommel matching her Quiet-Patroness pendant. Uses Charisma for attack and damage. Counts as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance.

Trina's pact focus — a small silver leaf-shaped pendant on a fine chain. The mark of the Quiet Patroness (Tasha / Iggwilv as the Archfey patron). Pulses faintly when she has cast or prepared a spell within the hour. Worn at her collar at all times.

Bonus Action. Summon a melee pact weapon of any kind into a free hand. The weapon counts as magical and uses Charisma for attack and damage rolls. Trina's pact form is a slim silver stiletto matching her leaf pendant.
When Trina casts Eldritch Blast, she adds her Charisma modifier (+5) to the damage of each ray it deals.
Trina sees normally in darkness, both magical and nonmagical, to a distance of 120 feet.

items.name: Eldritch Invocation: Mask of Many Faces

description.value: Trina can cast Disguise Self at will, without expending a spell slot.
Trina can cast Silent Image at will, without expending a spell slot or material components.
Trina can cast Hold Monster once per long rest as a Mystic Arcanum, without expending a spell slot.

A beam of crackling energy streaks toward a creature within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes 1d10 force damage. The spell creates more than one beam when you reach higher levels: two beams at 5th level, three beams at 11th level, four beams at 17th level. With Trina's Agonizing Blast invocation, each hit adds her Charisma modifier (+5) to the damage.
A spectral, floating hand appears at a point within 30 feet. The hand can manipulate objects up to 10 lbs, open unlocked doors and containers, retrieve items from open containers, etc.
Create a sound or an image of an object within range that lasts for the duration. The illusion ends if you dismiss it as an action or cast this spell again.
This minor magical trick performs a number of small, simple effects: light, clean, douse, mark, etc.

Concentration, up to 1 hour. Place a curse on a creature you can see. Until the spell ends, you deal an extra 1d6 necrotic damage to the target whenever you hit it with an attack. You also choose one ability when cast; the target has disadvantage on ability checks made with that ability.

materials.value: The petrified eye of a newt

description.value: You touch a willing creature who is not wearing armor. Until the spell ends, the target's base AC becomes 13 + their Dex modifier. The spell ends if the target dons armor or if you dismiss it as an action.
Bonus Action. Briefly surrounded by silvery mist, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

Concentration, up to 8 hours. Suggest a course of activity (limited to a sentence or two) and magically influence a creature you can see within range that can hear and understand you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or pursue the suggested course as best it can.

materials.value: A snake's tongue and a drop of honey

description.value: Concentration, up to 1 minute. Create a twisting pattern of colors in a 30-foot cube within range. Each creature in the area that can see the pattern must make a Wisdom save or be charmed for the duration. While charmed, the creature is incapacitated and has a speed of 0.

materials.value: A glowing stick of incense or a crystal vial filled with phosphorescent material

description.value: Reaction, when you see a creature within 60 feet casting a spell. Attempt to interrupt that spell. If the spell is 3rd level or lower, it fails automatically. If 4th level or higher, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability — the DC equals 10 + the spell's level. On a success, the creature's spell fails.

activation.condition: Seeing a creature within 60 feet cast a spell

description.value: You teleport yourself from your current location to any other spot within range. You arrive at exactly the spot desired. You can also teleport one willing creature with you, who must be within 5 feet when the spell is cast.

Concentration, up to 1 minute. Choose a creature within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom save or be paralyzed for the duration. The creature can repeat the save at the end of each of its turns.

materials.value: A small, straight piece of iron

blooming-rot-2.pact: The Quiet Patroness (Tasha / Iggwilv presented as Archfey muse — secret in setting)
